help.root:
Usage: gpuctl <command> [args]

Monitor
   list         Show GPU status and metrics

Tune (temporary, lost on reboot)
   tune get     Show tunable parameter ranges and current values
   tune set     Set GPU parameters
   tune reset   Reset GPU parameters to default

Config
   conf init    Generate default config file
   conf edit    Open config file in an editor
   load         Apply config file and reload service

Service
   health       Show service status

Other
   version      Print version information

Run 'gpuctl help <command>' for detailed usage.
help.list:
Usage: gpuctl list [-a] [-d <index,...>]

Show status and metrics for all GPUs.

Flags:
  -a, --all              Select all devices (default)
  -d, --device <index>   Device index, comma-separated (e.g. -d 0,1)
help.tune:
Usage: gpuctl tune <get|set|reset> [flags]

  tune get                    Show all tunable ranges and current values
  tune set param=value ...    Set GPU parameters
  tune reset [params...]      Reset parameters to default

Params (for set and reset):
  pl    / power_limit         Power limit (W)
  cogpu / clock_offset_gpu    GPU clock offset (MHz)
  comem / clock_offset_mem    Memory clock offset (MHz)
  clgpu / clock_limit_gpu     GPU clock limit (MHz)
  fan                         Fan speed (%)

Flags:
  -a, --all              Apply to all devices
  -d, --device <index>   Device index (required when multiple GPUs and no -a)

Examples:
  gpuctl tune get
  gpuctl tune set pl=100 -d 0
  gpuctl tune set cogpu=210 clgpu=2520 -a
  gpuctl tune reset fan -d 0
help.conf:
Usage: gpuctl conf <init|edit>

  conf init    Generate config file with current GPU defaults
  conf edit    Open config file in an editor

Config path is platform-dependent. Run 'gpuctl conf init' to see it.
help.load:
Usage: gpuctl load

Apply the config file to all GPUs and reload the running service.
Run 'gpuctl conf init' first if no config exists.
help.health:
Usage: gpuctl health

Show whether the gpuctl service is installed and running.
help.version:
Usage: gpuctl version

Print gpuctl version.
err.unknown_cmd:
gpuctl: unknown command '%s'
Run 'gpuctl help' for usage.
err.unknown_subcmd:
unknown subcommand '%s' for '%s'
Run 'gpuctl help %s' for usage.
err.no_subcmd:
'%s' requires a subcommand
Run 'gpuctl help %s' for usage.
err.no_gpus: no GPUs detected — cannot initialise config
msg.config_hint: Edit with 'gpuctl conf edit', then apply with 'gpuctl load'.
msg.conf_tpl_ignore: set value to null or ~ to ignore
msg.conf_tpl_fan_control: true: use fan curve; false: reset to vbios
msg.no_editor:
EDITOR is not set. Set it first, then run without sudo:
  EDITOR=nano gpuctl conf edit
msg.need_admin_edit:
Editing the config requires administrator privileges.
Please open the following file manually as administrator:
  %s
help.daemon:
Usage: sudo gpuctl daemon

Starts the background service that manages GPU settings and fan curves.
This command is normally invoked automatically by the system service manager
and does not need to be run manually.
err.need_root: requires root. Run with: sudo gpuctl conf init
msg.health_boot_not_enabled: Not set to start on boot.
msg.edit_as_root:
sudoedit requires a non-root caller.
Run without sudo, or edit the file directly:
  sudoedit %s
